description: Machine-readable conservative mapping from changed repository paths to validation targets. This registry drives ops/scripts/dev/select_validation_targets.py. It complements docs/testing/test-execution-targets.csv, test-execution-runs.csv, pitfall-index.csv, and agent-update-log.csv; it does not replace the ledger of observed runs.

defaults.path_matching: Repository-relative paths with forward slashes. Exact path entries also match descendants when they end with '/'. Glob entries use Python fnmatch semantics.

risk_levels.static: No runtime product behavior is exercised. Use for docs, tooling, encoding, formatting, and repository hygiene.
risk_levels.targeted: Focused test target for a bounded product or UI surface.
risk_levels.broad: Broad target that should be considered when shared harness, shared fixtures, auth, routing, or high-blast-radius files change.
risk_levels.full: Expensive full validation profile. Use when the changed surface makes target-only evidence too weak.

targets.description: UTF-8, mojibake-safety, whitespace, and docs/tooling hygiene checks for encoding-sensitive or documentation-only changes.
targets.notes: This target is intentionally recommended for docs and text-tooling diffs. It is not a substitute for product tests when source files change.

targets.description: Static validation for the diff-based validation selector and its machine-readable target registry.
targets.notes: First-version target; add a ledger entry after this selector is first committed and observed.

targets.description: Cross-platform static governance checks for production operator scripts, deployment helpers, and database bootstrap SQL.
targets.notes: This target does not execute Bash or contact services. Use it as a governance guardrail before Linux-only syntax/runtime checks.

targets.description: Static governance checks for aligned CI runtime baselines and canonical install/test commands across GitHub Actions and reference external pipelines.
targets.notes: Use this static target when CI entrypoints, runtime-version baselines, or CI validation docs change.

targets.description: Repository line-health metrics for structure, documentation, test-code, and primary-source trend checks.
targets.notes: Recommended when repository layout, tracking policy, or line-health tooling changes.

targets.description: Static validation for repo-local CourseEval skills and their lightweight agent metadata.
targets.notes: This intentionally avoids external PyYAML so skill sanity checks run in the normal repo environment.

targets.description: Static documentation-governance checks for required entrypoints, governance skill indexing, Markdown links, and likely stale path mentions.
targets.notes: Warnings for intentionally historical path mentions must be reviewed before treating the target as failed.

targets.description: Static boundary-governance checks for large files and suspicious backend import edges such as router-to-router imports.
targets.notes: Large-file findings are warnings and should usually become a documented follow-up unless a focused refactor task is in scope.

targets.description: Static structure-governance checks for root-file hygiene, duplicate root semantics, tiny docs, and safe structural cleanup.
targets.notes: This target uses Git ignore rules so private `.env`, `.agent-run`, and other ignored local artifacts are not treated as source layout.

targets.description: Static governance checks for CourseEval ORM model, bootstrap.ensure_schema_updates compatibility DDL, and no-Alembic schema documentation anchors.
targets.notes: This is a static governance guardrail. It does not replace PostgreSQL-backed migration or schema validation for release-quality claims.

targets.description: Static governance checks for FastAPI router registration, router prefixes, frontend API client bases, and API-surface documentation anchors.
targets.notes: This is a static guardrail for route/client/doc drift. It is not a complete OpenAPI export or a substitute for API regression tests.

targets.description: Static validation for local test-environment guardrails that diagnose pytest SQLite, process, and harness hazards without mutating local state.
targets.notes: This guardrail is read-only. It reports active pytest processes and the shared SQLite file state; it does not kill processes or delete test.sqlite.

targets.description: Focused backend API regressions for user profile appearance presets, personal style save/select/clear flows, and appearance-related public settings.
targets.notes: Use for appearance schema/router changes. This target is API-focused and does not replace school frontend visual checks.

targets.description: Focused backend API regressions for learning notes, note discussion metadata, and attendance date parsing used by embedded attendance/calendar workflows.
targets.notes: Pair with the targeted Playwright learning-notes/attendance tier when browser-visible behavior or routing is affected.

targets.description: Manual-script replacement smoke coverage for admin API routes through in-process FastAPI TestClient.
targets.notes: This target keeps former localhost manual smoke scripts represented in the validation selector. It is not a replacement for route-specific behavior/security tests.

targets.description: School SPA production build. Catches Vue/Vite import, syntax, bundling, and asset pipeline regressions before browser E2E is interpreted.
targets.notes: Recommended for school frontend source/config changes. A Playwright spec-only change does not necessarily require rebuild unless shared helpers or config changed.

targets.description: Node test coverage for admin Markdown/LaTeX preprocessing, clipboard fallback behavior, and Playwright preflight JSON diagnostics.
targets.notes: This target is intentionally cheaper than Playwright and should run before browser E2E for Markdown/LaTeX or copy-button changes.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright tier for learning-note, course-cover, teaching-calendar redirect, and attendance/calendar surfaces.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Does not replace full Playwright when shared E2E harness, auth, global setup, or broad routing changes.

targets.description: Focused backend attachment extraction and classification tests for text, Office, zip, and RAR handling.
targets.notes: If broader LLM grading, quota, or worker behavior changes, add LLM/homework behavior tests beyond this attachment-format target.

targets.description: Focused backend file upload/download API regressions for attachment response shape, upload compliance, stored-name downloads, and course cover-image upload reuse.
targets.notes: Use for bounded file schema/router changes. This target does not replace LLM attachment-format parsing tests when grading extraction behavior changes.

targets.description: Focused roster/user API target for admin-created student users, canonical Student binding, temporary-class backfill for missing class assignment, and user-to-roster synchronization.
targets.notes: Use when student accounts are created or edited through /api/users and must remain bound to canonical Student rows.

targets.description: Focused roster audit target for canonical Student identity and users.student_id migration prechecks.
targets.notes: Use before any student identity data migration or automatic repair. This target reports conflicts; it does not mutate data.

targets.description: Focused roster repair target for default/new-system student account data migration into canonical Student plus users.student_id bindings.
targets.notes: Use after the audit target when the change can mutate Student/User identity data. The repair path is scoped to new-system/default data, not broad historical migrations.

targets.description: Focused course/roster regression target for student account to roster synchronization, required-course enrollment sync, homework submission access, and class/course invariants.
targets.notes: Course access and roster changes often have broad effects. Consider behavior/security tests and PostgreSQL full validation for larger changes.

targets.description: Focused score/dashboard API target for subject-scoped teacher visibility, score composition, grade appeals, and course-owned dashboard stats/rankings.
targets.notes: Use this before full PostgreSQL fallback for bounded score/dashboard route changes. Escalate to security or full PostgreSQL validation for shared course-access helper, schema, or auth changes.

targets.description: Focused homework LLM grading pytest target for grading lifecycle, effective score behavior, retries, queue state, and submission serialization.
targets.notes: Pair with behavior and Playwright homework targets when browser-visible submission, review, or LLM routing behavior changes.

targets.description: Behavior pytest target for notification list/sync-status alignment, concurrent read-state updates, subject-scoped authorization edges, and target-scope regressions.
targets.notes: Use before or alongside notification header Playwright tiers when the invariant is mostly API/read-state behavior.

targets.description: Cross-feature behavior pytest target for homework lifecycle, LLM grading, score visibility, quota, and multi-actor transitions.
targets.notes: Recommended for cross-module homework/LLM changes that exceed a single focused backend file.

targets.description: Behavior pytest target for course, roster, homework, score appeal, and enrollment edge interactions.
targets.notes: Escalate to PostgreSQL full consideration when schema or broad course access helpers change.

targets.description: Focused backend coverage for default demo course seed data used by local installs and E2E reset helpers.
targets.notes: Use for default/demo seed data, seeded credentials, seeded courses, prefilled submissions, and demo roster construction. E2E dev route gate changes should also run backend.e2e_dev.api_hazard_tier.

targets.description: Fast TestClient hazard tier for /api/e2e/dev seed gates, mock LLM helpers, grading pump, and cross-role E2E-dev API edges.
targets.notes: This pytest target is usually cheaper and less fragile than Playwright when validating E2E dev API gates.

targets.description: Security regression pytest suite for unauthenticated access, role boundaries, admin-only routes, IDOR edges, invalid tokens, and abuse cases.
targets.requires_review_reason: Security runs are broad API guardrails; failures should be triaged before accepting targeted-only evidence.
targets.risk_escalation: Strongly recommended for auth, permission helper, course access, homework ACL, LLM admin route, or parent-code changes.
targets.notes: Does not replace full PostgreSQL validation for schema-sensitive authorization behavior.

targets.description: Self-organized WAI-VALID target for one-file E2E red-team samples covering auth rollback, same-context login races, selected_course cache poisoning, and parallel login isolation.
targets.requires_review_reason: Runs Playwright shards through the foreground WAI-VALID supervisor so each spec gets isolated API/UI ports and SQLite state; keep concurrency at 10 unless resource pressure is observed.
targets.notes: This target keeps red-team attacks as one spec file per sample while using WAI-VALID concurrent scheduling. Use it for auth/localStorage/session races and notification badge cache-poisoning changes.

targets.description: Small browser-backed security hardening slice for localStorage role tampering, direct student API mutation attempts, and E2E dev seed-token-only rejection on powerful endpoints.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Keep this spec small; broader API security coverage belongs in security.api_regression.

targets.description: Browser-backed parent portal hardening suite for parent-code login binding, same-class elective homework/notification leakage, and parent Vite runner startup.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports. This target also starts the parent portal Vite app.
targets.notes: Use for parent SPA validation. The spec runs from the school Playwright package but uses PLAYWRIGHT_PARENT_BASE_URL for the parent app.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright suite for discussion LLM assistant, long reply preview expansion, and course cover API/UI flows.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Escalate when shared E2E fixtures, route guards, or LLM seed helpers change.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright suite for homework submission previews, long comments, LLM regrade/recovery, multi-role API guards, and course cover flows.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Pair with backend homework/LLM pytest for API-heavy changes.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright suite for teacher stale-tab homework-appeal convergence, terminal-state conflict recovery, review/appeal interleave, and reload-safe non-actionable detail state.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use when homework appeal terminal-state recovery, stale teacher tabs, or detail-page appeal actions change. Pair with backend homework appeal pytest for API-only invariants.

targets.description: School Playwright smoke suite for login, seeded homework grid, teacher materials/notifications routes, and admin user grid.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Useful as a low-cost browser smoke when broad UI routing or auth surface changes, but not a replacement for route-specific suites.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright suite for student course UI, markdown reader/demo, sidebar controls, materials layout, and teaching-calendar redirect behavior.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use for browser-visible course reader, materials, markdown, or course-entry UI changes.

targets.description: API-heavy school Playwright tier for discussions validation, cross-class homework guards, LLM mock grading drain, and sync-status shape.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Keep this for the spec itself or when validating the browser-managed end-to-end composition. Most pure HTTP invariants should prefer backend.e2e_dev.api_hazard_tier or behavior pytest targets first.

targets.description: Focused Playwright/API guard rails derived from documented pitfalls and known risk themes.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use for edits to the pitfall guard spec itself. Prefer narrower backend pytest targets for pure API behavior changes.

targets.description: Responsive-layout Playwright guard rails for the school SPA mobile breakpoint and course-catalog containment.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use for edits to the responsive layout guard spec or layout changes that may affect mobile containment.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright suite for hard LLM homework scenarios, preset routing, quota edges, and repaired regrade flows.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use for browser-visible LLM routing and quota scenarios; pair with backend homework/LLM pytest for API-heavy implementation changes.

targets.description: Broad school Playwright resilience suite for stale state recovery, concurrent edits, enroll/drop idempotency, notifications, and appeal races.
targets.requires_review_reason: High-cost browser tier with concurrency coverage; run when resilience selectors or the covered stale-state invariants change.
targets.notes: Do not use as a cheap default for unrelated changes. Prefer narrower backend behavior tests when a browser stale-state path is not involved.

targets.description: Future advanced coverage Playwright scenarios 1-15 for stale tabs, concurrency, LLM failover, notifications, materials, attendance, and multi-actor races.
targets.requires_review_reason: High-difficulty Playwright scenarios are costly and environment-sensitive; run when touched invariants justify browser race coverage.
targets.notes: Do not use as a cheap default. It is recommended mostly when the spec/helper itself changes or broad race-prone surfaces are under review.

targets.description: Future advanced coverage Playwright scenarios 16-30 for material races, stale homework, LLM policy changes, parent/student visibility, notifications, and score overrides.
targets.requires_review_reason: High-difficulty Playwright scenarios are costly and environment-sensitive; run when touched invariants justify browser race coverage.
targets.notes: Do not use as a cheap default. It is recommended mostly when the spec/helper itself changes or broad race-prone surfaces are under review.

targets.description: Additive Playwright API/navigation checks for pagination boundaries, health, settings public, and course entry.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use for small API/navigation edges; prefer pytest for pure API contract expansion.

targets.description: Cross-cutting school Playwright edge suite for multi-role interleaving, token lifecycle, validation-boundary responses, and API-plus-UI convergence under concurrency.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Use when the spec itself changes or when auth, notification convergence, or multi-role browser/API edge handling needs a targeted browser check.

targets.description: API-heavy Playwright hazard tier for pagination 422 boundaries, LLM admin/student edges, parallel mark-all-read, E2E seed gates, forgot password, and disabled registration.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: The backend TestClient hazard target is usually a cheaper first check for /api/e2e/dev route changes.

targets.description: Targeted school Playwright suite for header notification badge, course switcher scoped sync-status, and focus-triggered convergence.
targets.requires_review_reason: Playwright targets depend on local browser, managed backend/frontend ports, seed/reset helpers, and should be run serially on default ports.
targets.notes: Pair with behavior.notifications.sync_api_edge for API-state changes.

targets.description: Deeper notification sync Playwright tier for admin global totals, course switch discipline, localStorage poison healing, concurrent publishes, isolation, mobile viewport, cold polling, delete races, multi-class course badge boundaries, admin-only global notification write scope, notification target-user denial, UI composer course/class scope, and explicit notification target-clearing updates.
targets.requires_review_reason: High-cost browser tier with concurrency and viewport coverage; run when notification UI/state convergence changes are material.
targets.notes: Use after narrower notification API/header targets when changes affect convergence or multi-role isolation.

targets.description: PostgreSQL-only pytest package for dialect-specific schema, constraint, and transaction behavior.
targets.requires_review_reason: Requires a live throwaway PostgreSQL database and TEST_DATABASE_URL; the selector does not provision it.
targets.notes: The selector can recommend this target but does not provision PostgreSQL. Use the local orchestrator or documented Postgres setup.

targets.description: Full pytest tree under PostgreSQL, intended for release-quality backend validation and zero PostgreSQL-only skips.
targets.requires_review_reason: Expensive environment-dependent full backend profile; requires PostgreSQL and supported attachment extractors before it can prove zero-skip validation.
targets.risk_escalation: Required or strongly recommended for schema, auth/security, DB reset, bootstrap, or broad backend changes.
targets.notes: Recommended for high-blast-radius backend, schema, test reset, behavior/security, or release-quality claims. It is expensive and environment-dependent.

targets.description: Full school Playwright E2E suite under apps/web/school. Recommended when shared E2E harness, Playwright config, global setup, seed/reset, auth, or broad frontend routing changes make targeted evidence too weak.
targets.requires_review_reason: High-cost browser profile with local browser, port, seed/reset, backend, and frontend dependencies; run serially unless isolated ports are configured.
targets.risk_escalation: Required or strongly recommended for Playwright harness, global setup, admin router, auth layout, seed/reset, or broad frontend navigation changes.
targets.notes: The current ledger records targeted Playwright but not a current full school Playwright run for this branch. Treat this as a high-cost recommendation, not an automatic command to start.

fallback_rules.description: Shared backend entrypoint, auth, config, bootstrap, schema, and E2E-dev gate changes require broad backend context beyond one targeted suite.
When all changed files are documentation or repository policy files, static checks are normally enough unless the docs claim runtime behavior changed.
Unmapped backend source changes should receive at least full PostgreSQL pytest consideration because the selector lacks a precise dependency graph.
School frontend source changes without a more specific Playwright target should receive full Playwright consideration after the build.
Any shared Playwright harness/config change should receive full Playwright consideration.
